title: New Delhi’s Air Turns Toxic, and the Finger-Pointing Begins
content: Schools and factories close. India’s Supreme Court blasts the government’s do-nothing response. But Delhi residents continue to suffer from the bad air.

more_info.byline: By Hari Kumar and Emily Schmall

en_title: New Delhi’s Air Turns Toxic, and the Finger-Pointing Begins
en_content: Schools and factories close. India’s Supreme Court blasts the government’s do-nothing response. But Delhi residents continue to suffer from the bad air.

title: The Women Bringing Sex Ed to the Arab World
content: Activists are using social media to do what Arab countries have failed to do: teach women about their bodies. They are aiming for nothing less than a cultural awakening.

more_info.byline: By Mona El-Naggar and Sara Aridi

en_title: The Women Bringing Sex Ed to the Arab World
en_content: Activists are using social media to do what Arab countries have failed to do: teach women about their bodies. They are aiming for nothing less than a cultural awakening.

title: Women’s Tennis Challenges China’s Narrative Over Missing Player
content: The WTA Tour chairman cast doubt over a reassuring email that state media claimed had been sent by Peng Shuai, who had accused a former top party official of sexual assault.

org_facet: Politburo Standing Committee of the Communist Party of China

en_content: The WTA Tour chairman cast doubt over a reassuring email that state media claimed had been sent by Peng Shuai, who had accused a former top party official of sexual assault.

title: Must This Swab Go That Far Up Your Nose to Test for Covid?
content: Some testers gently swab the front of your nostril and send you on your way. Others dig deep, bringing the pain. What’s the gold standard?

more_info.byline: By Livia Albeck-Ripka and John Yoon

en_title: Must This Swab Go That Far Up Your Nose to Test for Covid?
en_content: Some testers gently swab the front of your nostril and send you on your way. Others dig deep, bringing the pain. What’s the gold standard?

title: Intractable African Crises Flare as Biden’s Top Diplomat Visits Kenya
content: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken hoped to ease the turmoil engulfing Sudan and Ethiopia in his first trip to sub-Saharan Africa. Both worsened on his first full day.

more_info.byline: By Michael Crowley and Declan Walsh

title: Risk for Leader of Belarus: Migrants He Lured May Want to Stay
content: President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko of Belarus, whom the E.U. accuses of engineering a migrant crisis on the border with Poland, could face a serious headache if asylum seekers try to stay in his country.

title: Vaccinated vs. Unvaccinated: Europe’s Covid Culture War
content: In pockets of Europe, vaccine resistance has become the long tail of the populist nationalist movements that shook up European politics for a decade.

title: Mass Detentions of Civilians Fan ‘Climate of Fear’ in Ethiopia
content: An ethnically motivated detention campaign largely targeting Ethiopians of Tigrayan descent threatens to further unravel Africa’s second-most populous country a year into a civil war.
en_title: Mass Detentions of Civilians Fan ‘Climate of Fear’ in Ethiopia

title: Boris Johnson Battered in Parliament as Scandal Puts Him on Defense
content: Reversals and broken promises have eroded his party’s lead in the polls, and the prime minister faces a low-grade mutiny from Conservatives angry over a proposal to limit their options for extra income.

title: How the Belarus Standoff Is Unlike Recent Migrant Crises
